description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages build, test, analysis and coverage. Define a .build template that runs on saas-linux-small-amd64, uses before_script to print system info, creates obj/, then runs `cmake $CMAKE_OPTIONS $CMAKE_ADDITIONAL_OPTIONS .. && make -j$(nproc) && make -j$(nproc) install`, stores obj/ as on‑failure artifacts and runs for merge requests and branches. Extend this template in jobs such as fedora/build, .centos10, .centos9, alpine/openssl_3.x/musl, centos10s/openssl_3.5.x/x86_64, centos9s/openssl_3.5.x/x86_64, and .vs which adds Windows vcpkg setup before building with `cmake --build .` and `ctest`. Add analysis jobs like .csbuild, .fedora/memory-sanitizer, .valgrind, .fedora/address-sanitizer, fedora/abidiff, and coverity that run custom scripts (csbuild, clang sanitizers, make test_memcheck, ABI diff checks, Coverity scan) and publish on‑failure artifacts. Include test jobs .tests, .macos, .mingw, .tumbleweed that run `cmake $CMAKE_OPTIONS $CMAKE_ADDITIONAL_OPTIONS .. && make -j$(nproc) && ctest --output-on-failure` on appropriate images, use variable‑driven CMAKE options, cache vcpkg, and trigger with only/except rules for merge requests, branches, tags, schedules and specific refs.
